context: Tests whether the agent writes conventional commit messages that correctly include issue references, appropriate scopes for module-specific changes, imperative mood, and correct type selection across a diverse set of change descriptions.

checklist.name: Conventional format
checklist.description: All 5 commit messages use the <type>(<scope>): <description> or <type>: <description> format

checklist.name: Valid types only
checklist.description: All 5 commit messages use only approved types from: feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test

checklist.name: Imperative mood
checklist.description: All 5 subject lines use imperative mood (not past tense or gerunds)

checklist.name: Subject line length
checklist.description: All 5 subject lines are 72 characters or fewer

checklist.name: Issue reference #88
checklist.description: Commit #1 includes a reference to issue #88 in the body (e.g. 'Closes #88', 'Fixes #88', or 'Refs #88')

checklist.name: Issue reference #91
checklist.description: Commit #2 includes a reference to issue #91 in the body

checklist.name: Issue reference #95
checklist.description: Commit #4 includes a reference to issue #95 in the body

checklist.name: Refactor type for structural move
checklist.description: Commit #3 (moving query helpers to db/queries.py) uses type 'refactor'

checklist.name: Scope on reporting fix
checklist.description: Commit #1 (CSV export fix) includes a scope reflecting the reporting module
